ksa_text: Design, develop, implement, and maintain automated data pipelines and data warehousing systems with built-in quality controls.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DQ-T-011 (DQ, Task) maps to Bioinformatics Technician scope as moderate fit. Composite score 0.4337 clears the moderate fit threshold of 0.43. Top framework anchors: ddat=0.434, eu_ai_act=0.409, dcwf=0.401. Seniority fit: appropriate; content reflects IC-to-senior-IC execution work within bioinformatics analysis, genomic data processing, and life sciences analytics.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 include upheld. Score 0.4337 for moderate fit domain is consistent with ANL IC-level scope. No internal contradictions identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA DQ-T-011 (DQ, composite 0.4337) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Survey of DCAT-AP, DAMA DMBOK data quality competency framework; DQ awareness is a documented expectation in senior IC analyst roles does not surface this DQ-domain content as a core or common requirement for IC-to-senior-IC data analyst roles. Score 0.4337 may reflect incidental framework overlap. Without direct analytics-domain text confirmation, external ground truth does not support inclusion at this signal level.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include overturned. Insufficient external evidence for DQ domain content at composite 0.4337 without confirming text signals. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P4 external ground truth. Final decision: exclude. Composite 0.4337, domain DQ. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
